sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ambev inks deal to use AB InBev’s BEES B2B commerce platform
feed.summary: Ambev S.A. has formalized an agreement with its controlling shareholder, Anheuser‑Busch InBev, to continue utilizing and operating the BEES B2B digital commerce platform. This platform is integral for managing orders from retailers and small businesses across Ambev's markets, shifting customer ordering from manual processes to a digital, self-service model. Ambev will pay annual fees for access, with pricing benchmarked against similar digital commerce agreements, and the deal includes provisions for Ambev to participate economically if a third party acquires an interest in the entity owning BEES.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) CEO Sells $857,413.46 in Stock
feed.summary: Richard Hayne, CEO of Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN), sold 10,667 shares totaling $857,413.46 on December 22, 2025. The company's stock is currently trading at $77.24, with analysts holding a "Moderate Buy" consensus and a target price of $86.50.

feed.title: New ETF targets growth stocks across sectors and changing conditions
feed.summary: Virtus Investment Partners has launched the Virtus Silvant Growth Opportunities ETF (VGRO), an actively managed exchange-traded fund focused on identifying growth opportunities across various sectors. Managed by Silvant Capital Management, VGRO utilizes bottom-up fundamental research to build all-weather portfolios of secular and cyclical growth stocks, aiming to outperform in varied market conditions without a specific style or factor bias. This new ETF expands Virtus' multi-manager ETF platform, emphasizing that growth is a condition that any company can exhibit at some point in its lifecycle.

feed.title: FDA approves first treatment for often-fatal stem cell complication
feed.summary: The FDA has approved Omeros Corporation's YARTEMLEA (narsoplimab-wuug) as the first and only treatment for hematopoietic stem cell transplant-associated thrombotic microangiopathy (TA-TMA), an often-fatal complication. The approval is based on strong complete response rates and survival benefits observed in clinical trials and an Expanded Access Program, offering a significant breakthrough for adult and pediatric patients. Omeros plans a U.S. launch for YARTEMLEA in January 2026.

feed.title: Aflac reveals personal data of 22.6 million people stolen in cyberattack
feed.summary: Aflac has confirmed that a cyberattack in June 2025 resulted in the theft of personal data belonging to 22.65 million individuals, including customers, employees, and agents. The stolen information includes claims, Social Security numbers, and health information, prompting the company to begin notifying affected parties. The Scattered Spider hacking group, also linked to attacks on other insurance companies, is suspected to be behind the breach.

feed.title: Federal order extends NIPSCO’s R.M. Schahfer power station operation By Investing.com
feed.summary: Northern Indiana Public Service Company (NIPSCO) has been federally mandated to extend the operation of its R.M. Schahfer Generating Station for 90 days beyond its planned December 31, 2025, retirement. NIPSCO, a subsidiary of NiSource Inc. (NYSE:NI), will comply with the order, which likely stems from grid reliability concerns, while continuing its long-term energy strategy. NiSource, with a strong financial health score and consistent dividend history, reported Q3 2025 revenue above expectations and has several recent strategic developments and analyst upgrades.

feed.title: NiSource's Northern Indiana Plant Ordered to Operate Past Planned 2025 Retirement for 90 Days
feed.summary: NiSource's Northern Indiana Public Service Company (NIPSCO) has been ordered to operate its R.M. Schahfer Generating Station for an additional 90 days beyond its previously planned 2025 retirement date. This federal order will delay the station's decommissioning, impacting NiSource's energy transition plans. The company's stock, trading on the NYSE, saw a slight increase following this news.

feed.title: Kinder Morgan, Phillips 66 Launch New Open Season for Western Gateway
feed.summary: Kinder Morgan and Phillips 66 have concluded the initial open season for their proposed Western Gateway Pipeline, a refined products pipeline from Texas to Arizona, and plan to launch another open season next month. The project, targeting completion in 2029 with a 200,000 bpd capacity, received significant interest and shipper commitments, necessitating the new open season to offer remaining capacity and new destinations west of Colton, California. This collaborative effort will involve new pipeline construction and the reversal of existing lines to efficiently transport refined products from Mid-Continent refineries to markets in Arizona and Los Angeles.

feed.title: Intel Stock Slips After Report Says Nvidia Halted AI Chip Testing
feed.summary: Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) shares dropped after reports indicated that Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) had paused testing for manufacturing its advanced AI chips using Intel's 18A production process. This development is a setback for Intel's ambitions to become a major contract chipmaker, despite a previous $5 billion investment from Nvidia and ongoing progress in Intel's manufacturing technology. Intel's stock had previously seen significant gains due to various partnerships and government support.
